cycle_label: GOV-09 — governance-thread standalone sprint · ReconciliationPanel rework (bulk low-conf dismiss · reason capture · evidence-relevance hint · group-level bulk actions) · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-09 = 9). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-09 opened 2026-05-15 as the operator-quality-of-life follow-on to the GOV-07/08 findings: the ops dashboard's ReconciliationPanel.vue has the right SHAPE (per-confidence filter, grouping, bulk approve/dismiss, expandable evidence) but lacks four high-leverage ergonomics for walking a real 60+ suggestion queue. (1) No bulk 'dismiss all low-conf' action — operator must filter+selectall+dismiss in three clicks for what is recurring noise-floor cleanup. (2) The composable's dismiss already accepts reason but the panel's dismiss flow drops it — losing the audit trail (e.g., the Lorna RC6-rename false-positive dismissal had to be captured manually via curl). (3) No evidence-relevance hint on each row — the GOV-07 reconciler-confidence-deployment-scoped memory finding is invisible until each row is expanded, so the operator can't see at a glance when high-conf is just deployment-coincidence. (4) No group-level bulk actions — can't 'approve all high in this deployment' or 'dismiss all low in this deployment' for clusters with uniform action. GOV-09 lands all four in one loop, deploys darntech to CT 100, and codifies any UX-pattern findings that emerge.
goals: L01 — four-part ReconciliationPanel rework + deploy + close. PENDING. (1) Add a 'Dismiss all low-conf' header button that collects every low-confidence pending suggestion across all deployments and dismisses with a default reason. (2) Add a reason-capture flow on all dismiss paths (bulk + group + single) — quick-pick chips ('false positive — evidence doesn't match', 'stale — obsolete', 'duplicate', 'needs more evidence', 'other') + free-text override, wired through to the existing composable's reason parameter (which already exists; only the panel needs to start passing it). (3) Add per-row evidence-relevance hint — extract distinctive nouns from target_statement (length>4, lowercased, dedup), check intersection with concatenated evidence summaries; show a small badge: green check if overlap, amber warn if not. Surfaces the reconciler-confidence-deployment-scoped finding inline. (4) Add group-header bulk actions: 'Approve high (N)' if any high-conf in group, 'Dismiss low (N)' if any low-conf in group. Build, deploy to CT 100, verify on prod (walk a low-conf suggestion + a high-conf with content match + a high-conf without). Codify any UX-pattern finding (likely: 'memory findings without operator-facing tooling stay theoretical'). Close GOV-09.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: Proceed with GOV-09 L01: implement the four panel changes in darntech/src/components/project/ReconciliationPanel.vue (the composable already supports reason — only the panel needs updating), build via `npm run build`, deploy via `scp` to CT 100, verify the panel on https://ops.darrenarney.com /ops tab → Recon → Reconciliation view, codify any UX finding, close.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-09 opened 2026-05-15 as the operator-quality-of-life follow-on after the GOV-08 close + a partial walk of the (now 61) pending reconciliation suggestions. Operator flagged: 'looks like those kick over to the ops tab, that stuff I think we can rework a bit.' Selected all four rework options (bulk low-conf dismiss + reason capture + evidence-relevance hint + group-level bulk actions). No prior nephew owns ops-dashboard reconciliation UX; this is GOV-shaped operator-tooling work that closes the gap between the memory-codified findings (reconciler-confidence-deployment-scoped, denormalization-staleness-pattern) and the operator's day-to-day surface.
stakes: The ReconciliationPanel is where the operator actually decides which reconciler suggestions to accept. With 61 pending and the new memory finding that confidence labels are structurally generous (deployment-scoped, not content-aware), the panel either becomes the place where memory discipline gets applied — or becomes a place where operators give up and bulk-approve trusting the label. Closing the gap matters: the reconciliation_signal score moves when suggestions move, but it only moves CORRECTLY when those decisions are content-grounded. Same family as the GOV-07 fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation finding: a memory file is necessary but not sufficient; the surface that uses the data has to embody the same discipline. This loop is the tooling-side proof of that pattern.
notes: GOV-09 opened 2026-05-15, third sprint of the day (06/07/08/09 all same-day), darntech-only work_locus. Composable `useReconciliation` already passes `reason` to the API (dismissSuggestions(ids, reason?)); only the panel's dismiss handlers need to start collecting and passing it. Build process: `npm run build` in ~/projects/darntech, then `scp dist/* root@192.168.0.250:/var/www/darntech/`. Casey Junior deployment 09c22323. Related memory: reconciler-suggestion-confidence-deployment-scoped (the gap this loop's evidence-relevance hint addresses); fix-without-action-surface-reconciliation (the analogous discipline); denormalization-staleness-pattern (parallel surface-vs-memory discipline).
